content.text: I'll start by reading the context files to understand the task.

content.content:
1	{
2	  "phase": "question",
3	  "questions": [
4	    {
5	      "id": "q1",
6	      "question": "The task specifies a single file `ui-components.test.tsx` for all three components, but existing tests use one file per component (e.g., ErrorBoundary.test.tsx). Should we follow the task description (single file) or the existing convention (separate files)?",
7	      "category": "design",
8	      "why_it_matters": "Affects file organization and whether we create 1 or 3 test files. A single file deviates from the established pattern but matches the task spec."
9	    },
10	    {
11	      "id": "q2",
12	      "question": "Should `formatProbability` and `statusColor` utilities be mocked in the component tests, or should we use the real implementations? The format.test.ts already tests these utilities in isolation.",
13	      "category": "design",
14	      "why_it_matters": "Mocking keeps unit tests isolated but adds complexity. Using real implementations is simpler and the utilities are already tested separately. ErrorBoundary.test.tsx does not mock any utilities."
15	    },
16	    {
17	      "id": "q3",
18	      "question": "For ProbabilityBar, what edge cases should be covered? Candidates: empty outcomes array, missing marginal entries (defaults to 0), outcomes exceeding the 8-color palette (color wrapping), all-zero probabilities.",
19	      "category": "requirements",
20	      "why_it_matters": "The component handles these cases implicitly (nullish coalescing to 0, modulo on color index). Testing them ensures robustness but some may be over-testing for simple UI components."
21	    },
22	    {
23	      "id": "q4",
24	      "question": "Should Spinner's SVG structure (circle element, viewBox, animation keyframes) be tested via DOM queries, or is verifying it renders without crashing sufficient?",
25	      "category": "design",
26	      "why_it_matters": "SVG internals are implementation details. Testing them couples tests to markup. But the task says 'test visual output', which could mean verifying SVG attributes like width/height based on the size prop."
27	    },
28	    {
29	      "id": "q5",
30	      "question": "Do any of these components need `renderWithProviders` from the test helpers, or is plain `render` sufficient? ProbabilityBar, Spinner, and StatusBadge appear to have no context/router dependencies.",
31	      "category": "codebase",
32	      "why_it_matters": "Using plain `render` keeps tests simpler and faster. Using the wrong render function would either add unnecessary overhead or cause missing-provider errors."
33	    }
34	  ],
35	  "assumptions": [
36	    {
37	      "id": "a1",
38	      "assumption": "Plain `render` from @testing-library/react is sufficient - none of the three components use React context, routing, or query providers.",
39	      "confidence": 0.98,
40	      "if_wrong": "Tests would fail with missing provider errors; would need to switch to `renderWithProviders` from helpers.tsx."
41	    },
42	    {
43	      "id": "a2",
44	      "assumption": "We should use real `formatProbability` and `statusColor` implementations rather than mocking them, matching the ErrorBoundary.test.tsx pattern of not mocking dependencies.",
45	      "confidence": 0.85,
46	      "if_wrong": "Tests could become fragile if utility output format changes, but format.test.ts already covers that."
47	    },
48	    {
49	      "id": "a3",
50	      "assumption": "We should create a single file `ui-components.test.tsx` as the task description specifies, even though the codebase convention is one file per component.",
51	      "confidence": 0.9,
52	      "if_wrong": "Would need to split into three separate test files to match codebase conventions."
53	    },
54	    {
55	      "id": "a4",
56	      "assumption": "The MarketOutcome type is simply `{ id: string; name: string }` based on the types.ts definition, so test fixtures are trivial to construct.",
57	      "confidence": 0.99,
58	      "if_wrong": "Would need to add additional required fields to test fixtures."
59	    },
60	    {
61	      "id": "a5",
62	      "assumption": "Spinner's `size` prop should be tested by checking SVG width/height attributes, and LoadingPage should be tested by verifying it renders a Spinner (SVG element present).",
63	      "confidence": 0.9,
64	      "if_wrong": "May need different query strategies if SVG attributes aren't accessible via testing-library."
65	    },
66	    {
67	      "id": "a6",
68	      "assumption": "StatusBadge renders the status text in uppercase via CSS `textTransform`, so `screen.getByText` should match the original (non-uppercased) status string.",
69	      "confidence": 0.95,
70	      "if_wrong": "Would need to search for the uppercased version or use a case-insensitive matcher."
71	    }
72	  ],
73	  "spawn_children": false,
74	  "children": []
75	}
76

1	{
2	  "phase": "research",
3	  "answers": [
4	    {
5	      "question_id": "q1",
6	      "answer": "Follow the task description and create a single file `ui-components.test.tsx`. While the existing convention is one file per component, all existing test files live in `src/__tests__/` and are named after the thing they test. These three components are small, closely related UI primitives from the same `components/ui/` directory. A single grouped test file is a reasonable variation that matches the task spec. The convention of one-file-per-thing still holds - the 'thing' here is the UI components collection.",
7	      "confidence": 0.9,
8	      "evidence": [
9	        "frontend/src/__tests__/ErrorBoundary.test.tsx",
10	        "frontend/src/__tests__/format.test.ts",
11	        "frontend/src/__tests__/Portfolio.test.tsx",
12	        "frontend/src/__tests__/MarketDetail.test.tsx",
13	        "frontend/src/components/ui/ProbabilityBar.tsx",
14	        "frontend/src/components/ui/Spinner.tsx",
15	        "frontend/src/components/ui/StatusBadge.tsx"
16	      ],
17	      "uncertainty": "Team preference may differ - some teams strongly prefer one-file-per-component for test isolation and discoverability."
18	    },
19	    {
20	      "question_id": "q2",
21	      "answer": "Use real implementations of `formatProbability` and `statusColor`. No existing component test in the codebase mocks utility functions. ErrorBoundary.test.tsx uses plain render with no mocks. The page-level tests (Portfolio, MarketDetail, MarketList) mock the API client but never utility functions. format.test.ts already covers these utilities in isolation, so real implementations in component tests serve as lightweight integration verification.",
22	      "confidence": 0.95,
23	      "evidence": [
24	        "frontend/src/__tests__/ErrorBoundary.test.tsx:1-45",
25	        "frontend/src/__tests__/format.test.ts:1-40",
26	        "frontend/src/__tests__/Portfolio.test.tsx:3-5",
27	        "frontend/src/__tests__/MarketDetail.test.tsx:3-7"
28	      ],
29	      "uncertainty": "None - the pattern is clear and consistent across all existing tests."
30	    },
31	    {
32	      "question_id": "q3",
33	      "answer": "Cover these edge cases: (1) Basic rendering with multiple outcomes and marginals - the core happy path. (2) Missing marginal entries - the component uses `marginals[o.id] ?? 0` so missing entries default to 0%, verify this behavior. (3) Empty outcomes array - should render the container without crashing. Skip testing color wrapping beyond 8 outcomes and all-zero probabilities - these are pure implementation details (modulo arithmetic and formatProbability output) already covered by the utilities. Focus on the component's rendering behavior, not reimplementing unit tests for its dependencies.",
34	      "confidence": 0.85,
35	      "evidence": [
36	        "frontend/src/components/ui/ProbabilityBar.tsx:8-10",
37	        "frontend/src/components/ui/ProbabilityBar.tsx:14-30",
38	        "frontend/src/__tests__/format.test.ts:5-12"
39	      ],
40	      "uncertainty": "Whether empty outcomes array is worth testing - it's a degenerate case that may never occur in practice, but it's trivial to add."
41	    },
42	    {
43	      "question_id": "q4",
44	      "answer": "Test Spinner's SVG at a practical level: verify it renders an SVG element, and verify the `size` prop controls the width and height attributes. The SVG element has explicit `width={size}` and `height={size}` attributes, making them straightforward to assert via DOM queries. Do NOT test internal SVG structure (circle elements, stroke-dasharray, keyframe definitions) as those are implementation details. For LoadingPage, verify it renders an SVG element (proving a Spinner is present) - don't test Spinner internals again. This matches the ErrorBoundary.test.tsx pattern of testing observable behavior rather than internal markup.",
45	      "confidence": 0.92,
46	      "evidence": [
47	        "frontend/src/components/ui/Spinner.tsx:3-25",
48	        "frontend/src/__tests__/ErrorBoundary.test.tsx:15-43"
49	      ],
50	      "uncertainty": "Whether testing-library provides good SVG querying. SVG elements can be queried via `container.querySelector('svg')` and standard DOM attribute access, which is used in Analytics.test.tsx with `container.querySelector()`."
51	    },
52	    {
53	      "question_id": "q5",
54	      "answer": "Plain `render` from @testing-library/react is sufficient for all three components. ProbabilityBar imports only `formatProbability` (a pure function) and the `MarketOutcome` type. Spinner has zero external dependencies. StatusBadge imports only `statusColor` (a pure function). None of them use React context (QueryClient, Session), React Router hooks (useParams, useNavigate), or any other provider-dependent APIs. The existing ErrorBoundary.test.tsx confirms this pattern - simple components that don't need providers use plain `render()`.",
55	      "confidence": 0.99,
56	      "evidence": [
57	        "frontend/src/components/ui/ProbabilityBar.tsx:1-3",
58	        "frontend/src/components/ui/Spinner.tsx:1-25",
59	        "frontend/src/components/ui/StatusBadge.tsx:1-3",
60	        "frontend/src/__tests__/ErrorBoundary.test.tsx:1-3",
61	        "frontend/src/__tests__/helpers.tsx:1-30"
62	      ],
63	      "uncertainty": "None - component imports are definitive. No context or router hooks used."
64	    }
65	  ],
66	  "assumption_checks": [
67	    {
68	      "assumption_id": "a1",
69	      "verdict": "CORRECT",
70	      "confidence": 0.99,
71	      "impact": "No impact - plain `render` is confirmed sufficient. Component imports show no context, router, or query provider dependencies."
72	    },
73	    {
74	      "assumption_id": "a2",
75	      "verdict": "CORRECT",
76	      "confidence": 0.95,
77	      "impact": "No impact - using real implementations matches the established codebase pattern where only API clients are mocked, never utility functions."
78	    },
79	    {
80	      "assumption_id": "a3",
81	      "verdict": "CORRECT",
82	      "confidence": 0.9,
83	      "impact": "Minor deviation from one-file-per-component convention, but the task spec is explicit and these are small related UI primitives. Acceptable grouping."
84	    },
85	    {
86	      "assumption_id": "a4",
87	      "verdict": "CORRECT",
88	      "confidence": 0.99,
89	      "impact": "No impact - MarketOutcome is `{ id: string; name: string }` as assumed. Test fixtures are trivial: `{ id: 'yes', name: 'Yes' }`."
90	    },
91	    {
92	      "assumption_id": "a5",
93	      "verdict": "CORRECT",
94	      "confidence": 0.95,
95	      "impact": "No impact - Spinner SVG uses explicit `width={size}` and `height={size}` attributes, queryable via `container.querySelector('svg')` and standard DOM attribute access."
96	    },
97	    {
98	      "assumption_id": "a6",
99	      "verdict": "CORRECT",
100	      "confidence": 0.95,
101	      "impact": "No impact - CSS `textTransform: 'uppercase'` is a visual-only transform. The DOM textContent remains in original case, so `screen.getByText('active')` will match the rendered text."
102	    }
103	  ],

content.content:
1	[Task ui-components-001] PHASE 3a: PLAN
2	Title: Add tests for UI components
3	
4	Read .fleet/tasks/ui-components-001/questions.json and .fleet/tasks/ui-components-001/research.json
5	for context on what was asked and answered.
6	
7	YOU MUST create .fleet/tasks/ui-components-001/plan.json with this structure:
8	{
9	  "phase": "plan",
10	  "decisions": [
11	    { "id": "d1", "decision": "...", "based_on": ["q1"], "alternatives_rejected": ["..."], "reversibility": "easy|medium|hard" }
12	  ],
13	  "steps": [
14	    { "step": 1, "task": "...", "depends_on": [] }
15	  ],
16	  "spawn_children": false,
17	  "children": []
18	}
19	
20	DO NOT STOP until plan.json exists and contains at least one step.
21	Verify: ls -la .fleet/tasks/ui-components-001/ to confirm the file was written.
22	
23	DECOMPOSITION: Set spawn_children=true for independent sub-tasks that warrant
24	their own 4-phase workflow. Format: { "id": "...", "title": "...", "description": "..." }
25	
26	BEFORE YOU STOP: git add -A && git commit --no-verify -m "plan: ui-components-001"
